content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 27, 1989


Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Nielsen, Merksamer, Hodgson,
Parrinello & Mueller
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-89-634

Dear Mr. Robeck:

     We are in receipt of your letter dated October 25, 1989 (copy
enclosed) in which you request confirmation of telephone advice
provided to you on October 5, 1989. I advised you during our
telephone conversation of November 2, 1989 that upon
reconsideration of the advice provided to you and with a clearer
understanding of the question posed, we have concluded that we are
unable to confirm the telephone advice provided.  Because your
request for advice does not identify a particular committee, we
have treated your request as one for informal assistance.¹

QUESTION

     Is a contribution to a ballot measure committee controlled by
an elected state officer cumulated with contributions to candidate
committees controlled by that elected state officer for purposes
of the special odd-numbered year disclosure statement?

CONCLUSION

     Contributions to a ballot measure committee controlled by an
elected state officer must be cumulated with contributions to the
elected officer's other controlled committees for purposes of the
special odd-numbered year disclosure statement.


_________________________
     ¹ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

File No. I-89-634
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act (the "Act")² imposes certain
disclosure obligations upon candidate controlled committees.
Specifically, Section 84202.7 reads as follows:

(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b),
during an odd-numbered year, any committee by
virtue of Section 82013 which makes contributions
totaling five thousand dollars ($5,000) or more to
elected state officers, their controlled committees, or committees primarily formed to support or
oppose any elected state officer during a period
specified below shall file campaign statements on
the following dates:

(1) No later than April 30 for the period of
January 1 through March 31.

(2) No later than October 31 for the period
of July 1 through September 30.

(b) If a committee makes contributions totaling five thousand dollars ($5,000) or more to
elected state officers, their controlled committees, or committees primarily formed to support or
oppose any elected state officer during a period
specified in subdivision (a), and all of those
contributions are reported pursuant to Section
84202.5 or Section 86116 on or before the time
specified in subdivision (a), the committee shall
not be required to file additional statements for
that period pursuant to this section.

The special odd-numbered year report is triggered by
contributions to a candidate or any of his or her controlled committees, including ballot measure committees as well as candidate
committees. For purposes of determining the reporting threshold,
all contributions made by the committee to elected state officers,
their controlled committees, or committees primarily formed to
support or oppose any elected state officer must be cumulated.
Therefore, contributions to an elected state officer's controlled
ballot measure committees must be counted toward the $5,000
threshold for purposes of Section 84202.7.


2 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-89-634
Page 3

     Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact our Technical Assistance Division at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Blanca M. Breeze
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosures

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
SAN FRANCISCO

A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

October 25, 1989


Ms. Blanca Breeze
Attorney, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     I am writing to confirm telephone advice you provided
to me on Thursday, October 5, 1989. My understanding of the
question and the conclusion follows.

Question

     Is a contribution to a measure committee controlled by
an elected state officer cumulated with contributions to
candidate committees controlled by that elected state officer for
purposes of the special odd-year disclosure statement?
(Government Code section 84202.7.)

Conclusion

     A contribution to a measure committee controlled by an
elected state officer is not cumulated with contributions to
candidate committees controlled by that elected state officer for
purposes of the special odd year disclosure statements.

     Please contact me if I have misstated either the
question or the conclusion.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

BRUCE W. ROBECK
Paralegal Administrator
Political Reports Unit
Sacramento

BWR/mjsw
Bwr1trs\breezef.ppc

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


November 3, 1989


Bruce W. Robeck
Paralegal Administrator
Neilsen, Merksamer, et al.
770 L Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Letter No. 89-634

Dear Mr. Robeck:

You have requested confirmation of advice under the Political
Reform Act. We have reviewed your request and determined that it
is not appropriate for response with a simple confirmation.
Therefore, we will be preparing a fuller analysis of the issues
raised in your letter to us. After the analysis has been prepared
and approved, we will forward it to you as quickly as we can.

If you have any questions, you may contact me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

special odd-numbered year disclosure statement.
